Muppets Most Wanted (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $51,178,893Details
International Box Office $28,133,408
Worldwide Box Office $79,312,301
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,923,784
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,056,252
Total $11,980,036
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The entire Muppets gang goes on a global tour, selling out grand theaters in some of Europe’s most exciting destinations, including Berlin, Madrid and London. But mayhem follows the Muppets overseas, as they find themselves unwittingly entangled in an international crime caper headed by Constantine—the World’s Number One Criminal and a dead ringer for Kermit—and his dastardly sidekick Dominic “Number Two” Badguy.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$17,005,126 (33.2% of total gross)
Legs:3.01 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:64.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$55,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,194 opening theaters/3,194 max. theaters, 4.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $74,920,382

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 21st, 2014 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: March 28th, 2014 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
April 16th, 2014 (Wide) (Spain)
May 1st, 2014 (Wide) (Germany)
June 13th, 2014 (Wide) (Turkey)
June 26th, 2014 (Wide) (Brazil)
Video Release: August 12th, 2014 by Buena Vista Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some mild action.
(Rating bulletin 2293, 10/9/2013)
Running Time: 106 minutes
Franchise: Muppets
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Breaking the Fourth Wall, Character's Journey Shown on Map, Gratuitous Cameos, Intertitle, Invented Language, Prologue, Puppets in a Live-Action World, Scene in End Credits
Plot point: Backstage, C.I.A., Doppelganger, Falsely Accused, Heist, Interpol, Interspecies Romance, Mistaken Identity, Police Procedural, Prison Break, Talent Contest, Talking Animals
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Mandeville Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, German, Russian
